preamble: The transformation from a voiced palatal lateral approximant (/ʎ/) to a palatal nasal (/ɲ/) involves a shift in the manner of articulation, where the airflow changes from passing over the sides of the tongue to flowing entirely through the nasal cavity. This specific sound change has been observed in certain dialects, illustrating how palatal sounds can undergo nasalization.
phoneticEffects: This shift represents a change in manner of articulation from a lateral approximant to a nasal, while maintaining the palatal place of articulation

examples.note: The change of 'll' to 'ñ' (/ʎ/ to /ɲ/) has been documented in Cuban Bozal Spanish.

sources: Miguel, N. E. (2018). The art songs of Modesta Bor (1926-1998). University of Iowa.
